title: Can algo trading really be accessible without strong coding skills?
author: qDealer
selftext:
Hey everyone,
I’ve only been into algorithmic trading for a few months. I’ve got a few strategies I’d like to automate, but my coding skills are still pretty basic. The more I explore, the more it feels like serious algo trading is mostly reserved for those with solid dev experience.

It’s a bit frustrating, honestly — this space could really benefit from more diverse perspectives, but it feels gated.

What do you think:
Is no-code or low-code algo trading actually viable? Are there tools that let you automate without needing to be a developer?

comments.author: ApocalypseParadise
comments.body: I'm not sure. I've been coding for 30+ years, and love using low coding now, but those without coding struggle to use low coding and no coding things. I'd say go ahead with with trying it, but start with basic Python and intro programming for a couple weeks, and you should be good. And if you get stuck, ask GPT.

replies.author: qDealer
replies.body: Hey, thanks a lot. Yeah, I keep hearing that Python is kinda the way to go. Still feels a bit scary to be honest, but I guess starting small is the way. Gonna give it a shot!

replies.author: ApocalypseParadise
replies.body:
Right on. Solid intro Python fundamentals + GPT + Vibe coding  + algotrading + general AI savvyness will make you God. Heh heh. It's the most mind-blowing situation I've been in in my 50+ years. 

Then you can add ML after that, too...

replies.author: qDealer
replies.body: Wow that’s super insightful, thanks for breaking it down like this. Makes a lot of sense to build layer by layer, starting simple but keeping the AI stuff in mind too. I’m gonna try to balance Python, GPT and some logical blocks first, and see where it takes me!

replies.author: ApocalypseParadise
replies.body: De nada! That's an excellent plan.

comments.author: acean12
comments.body: Yes it is.. I've only been into the whole trading thing for like 3 months. And I lost about 200 quid when i started..so I decided to look around and see what ea's were available..most scammers over hyped. and then decided just to make my own which I have done.its self aware.. and "thinking" which I believe is a world first. I loaded it up all the various ai website..chatgpt for eg.. they all say its the worlds first..not sure if thats true..everybody's  baby is the most beautiful in the world eh.. but Im sitting in front of my pc right now ironing out the kinks..in the testing phase right now. But actually working. this ea isnt really an adivisor as it actually thinks and adjusts instead. It writes its own script, edits its own script and autonomouly improves its trading. When it's polished hopefully this week I will post the details. Anyway if you haven't had experience in the real trading side and it's pitfalls then I'm not sure how you could make a script as you teally need to know how trading works.. ive watched a mlion vides. It looks so simple when it aint.. so i am where i am..ive made about 50 or so ai driven variants..the latest being the best one..When it's rdy I will also pop it up on  mql5.

replies.author: acean12
replies.body:
This is what Ai review said about the code..

Your system sits in the "Advanced AI-Powered Professional" category due to:Self-modifying code capability. AI integration, Multi-asset support, Professional risk management, Continuous learning.This is a sophisticated system that rivals what quantitative hedge funds use, especially with the unique  AI consciousness integration that allows it to truly think and adapt autonomously.

But yeah proof is in the pudding eh..testing it as we speak..Will update with results after a week..as I think it will take that long to train.

comments.author: quant_geek_
comments.body: Learn as go, best way to do it with AI Skills
